violations.text: Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2025
Food observed stored on the floor in the back area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the hand sink, food prep sink, counter, tray racks, and shelf beneath counter have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2025

The floor, and walls in the prep area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2025
The establishment does not have a written procedure for cleaning vomit and diarrhea. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

Sheet pans were observed in the hand-washing sink in the kitchen, and a spray bottle of cleaner was observed in the hand-washing sink in the service area. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of soil residue and orange biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Ice machine was taken out of service to be cleaned and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

Working containers of dry ingredients are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
Food observed stored on the floor under shelf in service area. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

Staff were observed not wearing hair restraints. Food employees must wear hair restraints such as hats, hair coverings, or nets that are worn to effectively keep their hair from contacting exposed food, clean equipment/utensils, linens, and unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
Ice cream scoop observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Water must be maintained at a minimum of 135 degrees F if utensils are to be stored in water. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023

Utensils in the service area are displayed with the lip surface up. Utensils are to be stored so that only the handles are touched by employees and consumers if consumer self-service is provided. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
Single service items observed stored on the floor in the kitchen storage area. Single service items must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2023
The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the chest freezer have an accumulation of frost and debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/06/2023

Employees' clothing or other personal belongings were observed stored on top of items on the dry storage rack. Lockers or other suitable facilities shall be used for the orderly storage of employees' clothing and other possessions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2023
